entries.definition:
كتاب التاء

تنأ - تهم

(تيه) التاء والياء والهاء، كلمة صحيحة، وهي جِنْسٌ من الحَيْرَة والتِّيه والتّيهاء: المفازة يَتيه فيها الإنسان.

(باب التاء والهمزة وما يثلثهما )

revealed to Moses comprising the Pentateuch (the first five books
of the Bible) as distinct from الْإِنْجِيل ʾal-ʾinjīl (the Evangel, the Gospel,
the New Testament) (q.v. الْإِنْجِيل al-ʾinjīl) (3:65) يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ
تُحَاجُّونَ فِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَمَا أُنْزِلَتِ التَّوْرَاةُ وَالْإِنْجِيلُ إِلا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ
people of the Book,
why do you argue about Abraham when the Torah and the Gospel
were not revealed until after his time?

The Qurʾan states that these scriptures were subjected to
distortion and tampering (2:75-9; 4:46 and 5:15). The Qurʾan
also alludes to the fact that the Prophet Muḥammad’s advent was
predicted in these sacred scriptures (7:157).

تِين tīn [coll. n. with no verbal root thought to be a borrowing from a
sister Semitic language occurring once in the Qurʾān] fig(s) (95:1)
وَالتِّينِ وَالزَّيْتُونِ
by the fig and the olive; *التِّين name of Sura 95,
Meccan sura, so-named because of the reference to ‘Figs’ in
verse 1.

by the fig and the olive; *التِّين name of Sura 95,
Meccan sura, so-named because of the reference to ‘Figs’ in
verse 1.

entries.definition:
t-y-h expanse of desolate unmarked featureless desert;
wilderness; to lose one’s way, to be misguided; to be conceited,
to be obstinate. Of this root, يَتِيهُون yatīhūn occurs once in the
Qurʾān.

يَتِيه yatīh [imperf. of v. تَاهَ tāha, intrans.] to wander around
having lost one’s way (5:26) قَالَ فَإِنَّهَا مُحَرَّمَةٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً يَتِيهُونَ فِي
الْأَرْضِ
He said, ‘It [the holy land] is forbidden to them for forty
years, [they will] wander in the land [being lost].’

# VERBATIM 

ث / thāʾ

الثاء ʾal-thāʾ the fourth letter of the alphabet; it represents a voiceless
inter-dental fricative sound.
